title: Thelypteris sandsii
taxonomy: Thelypteris sandsii is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which consists of various species of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The species is native to tropical regions and is often found growing near water sources. Thelypteris sandsii is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, which includes a diverse group of ferns. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species within the family.

intro: Thelypteris sandsii, also known as Sands' fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris sandsii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
